title: GLM 5.2 发布
source_name: Hacker News 热门（buzzing.cc 中文翻译）
full_text:
Posts from X
Most Activity Most Activity Timeline
VIEWS 121.7K BOOKMARKS 332
kache @yacineMTB
Coldness be my god. I'm gonna roll over and die, surrounded by tmux sessions streaming a thousand tokens per second, optimizing my CUDA kernels in Chinese
Good night everybody
jietang @jietang
GLM-5.2 is Fully Open, Frontier Intelligence Belongs to Everyone
Today, the sudden restriction of certain frontier models is deeply regrettable. At a time when access to frontier models is abruptly cut off for non-technical reasons, we are even more convinced of one thing: science should be global.
The path to AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) must never be enclosed by high walls. We have always believed that AGI should be the cornerstone for all of humanity to collaboratively explore the boundaries of intelligence and solve complex challenges, rather than a privilege monopolized by a few rules and subject to revocation at any moment. In the face of external blockades and restrictions, our attitude is one of radical openness. Frontier intelligence must remain open-source, accessible, and buildable, serving every dedicated developer.
GLM-5.2 is Zhipu's most capable open-source model to date. It not only supports a truly usable 1M context window but also maintains a continuous lead in the independent completion of long-horizon tasks, providing solid foundational support for building complex agent applications. It also continues to be our main engine for creating the strongest domestic coding model.
Tonight at 5:21—at this special moment—GLM-5.2 will officially be available to all GLM Coding Plan users (including Lite / Pro / Max). The API will also go live next week.
A step closer to frontier intelligence for everyone.
The future of AI is open, and it is for the people.
ModelKey: GLM-5.2
Expand post
13h | Views 121.7K Likes 1.3K Bookmarks 332
LIKES 1.4K RETWEETS 70 REPLIES 62
Chubby♨️ @kimmonismus
The US government's decision was arguably the biggest PR boost for open source ever.
Perhaps this will be retaliation, and Chinese open-source models can now position themselves as freedom software.
Z.ai @Zai_org
Intelligence should be open, accessible, and ready to build with, empowering every developer, everywhere.
GLM-5.2 is now available to all GLM Coding Plan users, including Lite, Pro, Max, and Team plans.
http://docs.z.ai/devpack/latest-model
As our new flagship model, GLM-5.2 delivers powerful coding capabilities, usable 1M-context support, and continued strengths in long-horizon tasks.
API and Chatbot services will launch next week. The model will also be officially open-sourced next week under the MIT License.
The future of AI is open, and it belongs to the people.
Expand post

14h | Views 74.6K Likes 1.3K Bookmarks 100
Deli Chen @victor207755822
A good business model should not turn your heaviest users into a source of loss.
This is exactly why I have always believed that a “coding plan” (usage‑based pricing) is a terrible business.
Think of a gym that only makes money because people buy memberships and then never show up.
That’s a broken logic.
A sound business model should ensure that your most core, highest‑volume users generate the largest profit – not the other way around.
#BusinessModel #StartupTruth #UsageBasedPricing #BadEconomics

19h | Views 16.5K Likes 331 Bookmarks 32
Z.ai @Zai_org
GLM-5.2 supports two thinking-effort levels: High and Max.
For coding tasks, we recommend using Max effort to enable deeper reasoning and more reliable performance.
20h | Views 2.5K Likes 67 Bookmarks 1
Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞) @teortaxesTex
Obviously DeepSeek tends to ignore these dynamics, but I wish to see how much ground 4.1 covers in one step. Their objective, after all, was explicitly "fast iterations", and it's been 7 weeks since V4-Preview.
Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞) @teortaxesTex
GLM and Kimi are on the third iteration of their flagships. They too are in the fast update cycle.
19h | Views 2.7K Likes 41 Bookmarks 1
Paweł J Lisowski @PawelJLisowski
Its massive boost for them. But we have massive issue on hardware side. If US gov bans nvidia and others from exporting hardware and bans inference providers from selling it to foreigners too, then where are we? EU would be giga screwed. Only China is starting to produce chips on relevant scale and their demand is too high to export it anyway.
We are so cooked in europe
14h | Views 448 Likes 4 Bookmarks 1

seems contradictory
no weights or even at least API

Alex J. Champandard 🌱 @alexjc
It's a good approach to releasing...
Launch Saturday to avoid disruption to businesses relying on the service weekdays; mid-day launch local time to have engineers monitoring; released to Coding Plan first for testing & feedback; encourage people to subscribe to run benchmarks.
Z.ai @Zai_org
Intelligence should be open, accessible, and ready to build with, empowering every developer, everywhere.
GLM-5.2 is now available to all GLM Coding Plan users, including Lite, Pro, Max, and Team plans.

It also continues to be our main engine for creating the strongest domestic coding model.
Tonight at 5:21—at this special moment—GLM-5.2 will officially be available to all GLM Coding Plan users (including Lite / Pro / Max). The API will also go live next week.
A step closer to frontier intelligence for everyone.
The future of AI is open, and it is for the people.

Deli Chen @victor207755822
A good business model should not turn your heaviest users into a source of loss.
This is exactly why I have always believed that a “coding plan” (usage‑based pricing) is a terrible business.
Think of a gym that only makes money because people buy memberships and then never show up.
That’s a broken logic.
A sound business model should ensure that your most core, highest‑volume users generate the largest profit – not the other way around.
#BusinessModel #StartupTruth #UsageBasedPricing #BadEconomics
